content.text:
Module:
Data Analysis  |
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEM1
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
0  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental concepts of data analysis
Develop skills in data exploration, cleaning and transformation
Understand and apply various statistical and predictive analysis techniques
Use data analysis and visualization tools
Interpret analysis results and communicate them effectively
Recommended Prerequisites
Algebra, descriptive statistics, and probability
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Data Analysis
Review: linear algebra, probability, and descriptive statistics
Definition and challenges of data analysis in information systems engineering
Data types (structured, semi-structured, unstructured)
Steps in the data analysis lifecycle
Data Exploration and Preparation
Data exploration techniques (visualization, descriptive statistics)
Data cleaning (handling missing values, error detection and correction)
Data transformation (normalization, aggregation, new variable creation)
Descriptive and Inferential Statistical Analysis
Descriptive statistics (mean, variance, correlation, etc.)
Hypothesis testing (t-tests, ANOVA, chi-square)
Linear and logistic regression
Predictive Analysis Techniques
Decision trees and random forests
Support vector machines
Neural networks
Time series models
Classification Methods
Supervised and unsupervised classification
Partitioning methods
Hierarchical ascending classification
Assessment
Semester exam (in-person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — TP tests, personal work
Practical Work Notes
Use of the Python language is recommended
Students will discover data analysis tools: pandas, TensorFlow, etc.; and visualization tools: matplotlib, seaborn, tableau, etc.
Bibliographic References
Petrelli, M. (2021). Introduction to Python in Earth Science Data Analysis: From Descriptive Statistics to Machine Learning. Springer Nature.
Provost, F., & Fawcett, T. (2013). Data Science for Business. O'Reilly Media.
DasGupta, A. (2011). Probability for statistics and machine learning: fundamentals and advanced topics. Springer.
Montgomery, D. C., & Runger, G. C. (2010). Applied Statistics and Probability for Engineers. John Wiley & Sons.
Nathan Yau (2013). Data visualization. Editions Eyrolles.
Pierre Rigollet (2013). Analysez efficacement vos données. Editions Eni.
VanderPlas, J. (2016). Python Data Science Handbook: Essential Tools for Working with Data. O'Reilly Media.
Brigitte Escofier, Jérôme Pagès (2016). Analyses factorielles simples et multiples. 5e édition. DUNOD.
